blocks.content: Larry stood there. Three seconds. Five. Long enough for it to become a thing.
A sophomore bumped his shoulder going past and didn't apologize. He drifted to a table near the back and sat down.
He was halfway through his food when one of the juniors from that morning slid into the seat across from him.

blocks.speaker: Junior
blocks.content: I can see that. You should go over there.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know what table it is.
The junior shrugged and left. At the center table Jonathan was mid-story, hands moving, the whole room leaning in. He didn't scan for Larry again.

blocks.text: is going to have to figure something out.

blocks.prompt: What does Larry do for the rest of lunch?

options.label: Watch Jonathan. Figure out how he does it.
Look busy. Don't let it show.
